articles.title: Araghchi meets Hamas and Hezbollah delegations in Tehran
articles.summary: Araghchi meets Hamas and Hezbollah delegations in Tehran Iranian foreign minister Abbas Araghchi held separate meetings in Tehran with delegations from Hamas and Hezbollah, who were in the Iranian capital to attend funeral ceremonies for former Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei. According to Iran’s foreign ministry, the Hamas delegation, led by Mohammad Darwish, offered condolences and discussed conditions in Gaza and the occupied West Bank, while a Hezbollah delegation headed by senior official Muhammad Fneish conveyed condolences from the group’s leader Naim Qassem. articles.summary: Turkey eyes US green light for fighter jet engines at NATO summit US President Donald Trump's visit to Ankara for the NATO summit could help secure Turkey's acquisition of dozens of fighter jet engines, but won't resolve the F-35 dispute that has soured ties, analysts have told AFP. The July 7-8 summit will bring together leaders from the military alliance's 32 member states. Iraqi cities of Najaf and Karbala to receive Khamenei’s remains
articles.summary: Iraqi cities of Najaf and Karbala to receive Khamenei’s remains The week-long funeral for former Iranian Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei will extend beyond Iran, with ceremonies scheduled in the Iraqi cities of Najaf and Karbala later this week, according to Al Jazeera. After public mourning events in Tehran and a commemoration ceremony in Qom, Khamenei’s remains are expected to be flown to Iraq on Wednesday. Ceremonies will be held in Najaf and Karbala, home to the shrines of Imam Ali and Imam Hussein, before the body is returned to Iran for burial in Mashhad at the Imam Reza shrine.  Mourne

articles.summary: Morning update Millions of mourners gathered in Tehran for funeral prayers for former Iranian Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei as ceremonies entered their second day. articles.summary: Tehran metro records seven million trips for Khamenei funeral Tehran’s metro system recorded more than 7 million passenger trips between Saturday evening and Sunday morning as large crowds travelled to attend funeral ceremonies for former Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei, according to figures cited by Tasnim News Agency. The funeral at Tehran’s Imam Khomeini Mosalla is continuing throughout the day, with organisers urging attendees to use public transport. articles.summary: Israeli minister confirms Iron Dome battery deployed to UAE during war Israel deployed an Iron Dome battery to the UAE early in the war with Iran, Israeli media reported. Israel’s transportation minister Miri Regev told Army Radio, “They understood that the ballistic missiles were one of the biggest challenges.”  The Times of Israel reported that Regev is the first Israeli official to publicly state that the missile defence system was sent to the UAE after Axios first reported the move in April. articles.summary: Funeral prayers held for Khamenei’s infant granddaughter killed in US-Israeli strike Funeral prayers were also held for four members of Ali Khamenei’s family who were killed in the joint US-Israeli strike on 28 February, including his 14-month-old granddaughter, Zahra Mohammadi Golpayegani. Footage published by Tasnim news agency showed the infant’s coffin placed beside Khamenei’s during the ceremony. Khamenei’s sons attend funeral prayers in Tehran
articles.summary: Khamenei’s sons attend funeral prayers in Tehran The Associated Press reported that Ali Khamenei’s sons Masoud, Meysam and Mostafa attended funeral prayers for the late Iranian supreme leader in Tehran on Sunday. The ceremony was also attended by Ahmad Vahidi, commander of the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps. articles.title: Funeral ceremonies continue for Khamenei, senior officials join
articles.summary: Funeral ceremonies continue for Khamenei, senior officials join Funeral prayers were held for Iran’s late Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei on Sunday at Tehran’s Grand Mosalla complex, marking the second day of ceremonies that have drawn large crowds to the capital. President Masoud Pezeshkian, parliament speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf and other senior officials attended the prayers, which were led by prominent Shia cleric Jafar Sobhani. articles.title: Fewer Ships Transiting Hormuz Along Oman Coast After U-Turns
articles.summary: The number of vessels sailing through the Strait of Hormuz along the Omani coast fell to a trickle on Sunday, a day after several exiting along that route made sharp reversals, underscoring Iran’s continued efforts to maintain its grip on the waterway.

IRNA reported that Brigadier General Ahmad Vahidi, Commander-in-Chief of Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC), who made his first public appearance on Friday after months of absence from the public eye, was among those who offered the prayers. articles.title: Video: Israeli settlers storm Hebron's Old City under protection of Israeli forces
articles.summary: Video: Israeli settlers storm Hebron's Old City under protection of Israeli forces Illegal Israeli settlers entered Hebron's Old City under the protection of Israeli forces, with footage showing heavily armed soldiers clearing Palestinian residents from streets and public areas to allow the settlers to move through the area. Videos from the scene show settlers roaming the Old City while Israeli forces imposed restrictions on local Palestinian movement. The incident comes amid growing concern over expanding illegal settler activity and tighter Israeli control in Hebron's historic centre. Illega

articles.title: Pakistan to host new round of US-Iran talks, report says
articles.summary: Pakistan to host new round of US-Iran talks, report says Pakistan is expected to host a new round of talks between the United States and Iran on July 11, according to Saudi state-owned broadcaster Al Arabiya, which cited unnamed sources. The report said the discussions are expected to focus on US sanctions on Iran, frozen Iranian assets and Tehran’s nuclear programme. Al Arabiya added that the composition of the Iranian delegation has not yet been finalised and will be decided after the funeral of former Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei.

articles.summary: Two Palestinians killed, several injured in Israeli drone strikes in Gaza Palestinian news agency WAFA reported that a Palestinian was killed after an Israeli drone struck a group of people near the Asqula junction, east of Gaza City. Another strike killed one more Palestinian and injured several others, some critically, in an Israeli drone attack near the Abu Sharkh roundabout, west of Jabalia refugee camp in the northern Gaza Strip, WAFA reported. articles.title: More than 140 Palestinian citizens of Israel killed this year
articles.summary: More than 140 Palestinian citizens of Israel killed this year More than 140 Palestinian citizens of Israel have been killed so far this year, a 12 percent increase from the same period in 2025, according to data cited by AFP from the Abraham Initiatives, an organisation that promotes coexistence between Jewish and Arab communities. The deaths have been linked largely to criminal gangs, family feuds, widespread access to firearms and what many community members describe as inadequate police enforcement. articles.title: OPEC+ tipped to raise quotas again as Middle East calms
articles.summary: OPEC+ tipped to raise quotas again as Middle East calms Seven OPEC+ members are likely to raise oil production quotas on Sunday as Gulf countries are reeling from the Middle East war, analysts told AFP. Saudi Arabia, Russia and five other members of the enlarged Organisation of the Petroleum Exporting Countries (OPEC+) are meeting online on Sunday to discuss quotas for August. The newspaper, citing sources familiar with security assessments, reported that officials feared Ben Gvir could face large demonstrations and legal challenges in the United States. articles.title: Ship-tracking firm says four ships changed course in Hormuz
articles.summary: Ship-tracking firm says four ships changed course in Hormuz Four vessels attempting to transit the Strait of Hormuz via the route along Oman’s coast have altered course, ship-tracing firm Windward said. In a post on X, Windward said two of the vessels reversed course and the other two diverted to a central corridor. Iran opposes ships using the southern route through Oman’s territorial waters and has previously attacked at least two vessels travelling that corridor.  The attacks triggered retaliatory attacks on Iranian military assets by the US in and around Hormuz. articles.summary: Trump says Netanyahu ‘knows who the boss is’ US President Donald Trump said he and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu “get along very good” and added that the Israeli leader “knows who the boss is” in a phone interview with Axios. Trump also confirmed that he expects to meet Netanyahu soon after the Nato summit in Turkey, saying the Israeli prime minister had requested a meeting at the White House.
